title: Work form use for job application submission and tracking
excerpt: If you’ve used Workforms to collect job applications, applicant tracking, posted the form link to multiple platforms (LinkedIn, Indeed) for an open position give me your feedback, pros, cons, etc. please.

posts.author: jenniferalp (Jennifer Pineda)
posts.cookedHtml: If you’ve used Workforms to collect job applications, applicant tracking, posted the form link to multiple platforms (LinkedIn, Indeed) for an open position give me your feedback, pros, cons, etc. please.

posts.author: Racingleys (Alek)
posts.cookedHtml: I had a much easier time polishing my own medical CV after browsing the layouts at Free Medical CV Templates | Professional Healthcare Resume Templates | Modern CV because seeing different styles side by side helped me figure out what actually fit my experience. If you’re stuck choosing a format or just want something cleaner than a basic Word doc, that page gives you a bunch of ready-to-use options.

posts.author: YOGESHP (Yogesh Pokale)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @jenniferalp

I helped our hiring team set up boards with Workforms to collect applications and track candidates, and overall it worked well for a straightforward hiring workflow. Applications submitted through the form landed directly in the board, which made it easy for the team to review candidates and move them through stages.

We also added a few automations for example, sending a notification/confirmation to the applicant after submission and notifying the hiring team when a new application came in. That helped keep things moving without manual follow-ups.

One thing we noticed is that if the same form link is posted on multiple platforms (LinkedIn, Indeed, etc.), it’s harder to track the source unless you add a “How did you hear about this role?” field. And if the applicant volume gets high, the board can become a bit heavy to manage compared to a dedicated ATS. But for smaller hiring needs, it worked nicely.
